reviews.review_msg: I had no problems with this professor. We did have to purchase her specific publication of the textbook for this course and had to read 1 play a week which was pretty time consuming (about 2-3 hours) I also noticed that she included information on the tests that she off handedly mentioned in class (not on the slides), attendance was for tutorial
i love her bc shes like so raw and unfiltered like she literally relates to us even shes 42 years old. she has an amazing sense of humor and she gives really good feedback.

Amazing professor. As an immigrant, I was always scared of English classes but she made it very doable. She helps you out to understand the essay prompt, and helps you brainstorm ideas and create your outline. She is super nice and accessible outside of class. The final was not essays, but rather short questions. Best Prof ever !!

Diana is a great prof in general, was passionate during lectures and even gave me an extension for TSIS quizzes since I made a mistake doing every single one. Unfortunately as a science student I didn't have a very good experience during the course because it's english but that doesn't change the fact that she's a great prof.

HANDS DOWN THE BEST ENGLISH PROF EVER. Dr. Solomon is an amazing lecturer and she is quite passionate about literature. As a science student who doesn't take English classes, I deeply enjoyed this class. Dr. Solomon Shows so much care and respect for her students and explains assignments, papers and exam criteria super clearly. 100% recommended.

Dr.Solomon is the best English professor out there. To be very honest if you go to her lectures and pay attention you should do just fine in the class and even if you dont pay enough attention she has recordings. Final exam is all her notes she aint tryna trick you. 100% recommend. my TA was a tough marker but Dr.Diana was so so so awesome.

Professor Diana is an amazing professor!! Her lectures are full of exciting topics. We had to read 4 novels this semester, which were not terrible. The final exam was extremely fair as everything she said in the Lecture was in the exam. I wish more profs were like her, it would make learning so much fun. Lastly, my TA was amazing!!

Unprofessional. In her ENG 398 class we required 3-4 books for her class when I asked if she could put the books on reserve at the library she told me no & asked me y I didnt buy the texts, when I told her I couldnt afford it she asked me what major Im in & dont textbooks cost more when I told her theyre free she just told me to figure it ou

Dr. Solomon is extremely clear in what she expects while also being fair in every situation. Her lectures were interesting both in their content and the form by which she presented them. In almost everyway she was giving us tools to learn from. I was not necessarily looking forward to this course, but I found a lot of enjoyment because of her.

Professor Solomon is a really great prof that I'd highly recommend! Her lectures were organized and she made it easy to recognize the key points of each reading. Our grade is only based off of 1 essay and the final, so the workload was nothing compared to other classes. Also very approachable!

Diana Solomon is a good ENGL113W professor. She genuinely cares for the success of her students. Attendance is mandatory because she doesn't post the lecture slides on canvas. There's only 2 1000-word papers, so the workload is pretty low. The final is quite easy too - NO ESSAY QUESTIONS! I didn't get lucky enough with my TA, so pray for a good one

Diana is an amazing professor who shows passion in what she teaches. Her lectures are very interesting and are a must, as most things learned in class are on the final. The TA's were amazing too, and the class had 2 essays and a final. The final was really easy, as Diana wants her students to succeed and get a good mark. I would recommend her!

Dr. Solomon animates the 18th century in her own quirky way. The textbook will be used 100%. Miss a class? That's okay. Dr. Solomon records all of her lectures that are great for review. She won't pull any surprises for exams. I would take a class with her again.

Being a science major, I hate English, yet somehow Dr. Solomon's lectures are so great that I magically like drama plays now. Definitely take classes with her if you lack a passion for English and are just taking this for an elective. She will change the way you think. Her final was extremely easy, although the papers were marked more harshly.

ENG 113 was my favourite class this semester! Diana does her best to make the reading material and her lectures interesting.  I highly suggest attending as many lectures as possible, I find the final exam has a lot of things mentioned only in lecture.

I seriously recommend this class for first years or anyone looking for an easy mark! minimal reading and the papers were moderately easy and the final was matching and fill in the blank, NO ESSAY!! Diana was funny and helpful and made it easy for students to do well in the class.

Diana is awesome! Funny, knows her stuff, cares about your thoughts, & loves teaching. Expects you at every class, prepared. Sets you up to succeed. Gave 2 free missed classes meant to cover illness/emerg. Is reasonable. Took class as loved her in Engl205! She studies comedy & 18th ct. lit. - was interesting. Also helped us with writing immensely.
she's nice as a person, but that does not make her a good prof. unclear directions and even if you go see her out of class she's no help.

Amazing professor. Her lectures are engaging and highly entertaining as she manages to infuse humor in all that she teaches. The texts themselves were very enjoyable reads, and also immensely funny. The most enjoyable class I've taken, the TA was also extremely helpful. If you can only take ONE English course, make sure it's one she is teaching.

She's really friendly, and I enjoyed her lectures. She marks harder on the essays, but her exams are really easy. She's really sympathetic towards your learning situations and tries to make the course best for you. I hope she makes the course more in-depth to the texts, but considering this is only a 200lvl course, it's not a problem.

Loved this prof and loved the class! I finished off my last 200-level credit with her and I already can't wait to take a 400-level with her. She gives clear, fair feedback. The exams were the easiest part of the course as long as you showed up to lecture and studied. Overall an enjoyable class with a great prof who is approachable and friendly :)

Dr. Solomon is probably the best prof I had at SFU! Her lectures are interesting and don't worry if you're not into plays because she does a good job of explaining what is happening. I didn't read much plays or literature before her class but I still found the class still interesting. This class is definitely an easy A!

Lectures are more interesting than English professors. Ended up not going to half of them but studied hard for the final by listening to almost all lecture recordings. Received 96% on final by doing this so either go to class and take good notes or listen later.  Reading the books is not necessary as long as you can provide evidence for ur papers.

Diana was amazing! Extremely helpful with her feedback and her lectures are never boring! She loves the Oxford English Dictionary so always use that in your analysis. 3 words: Analysis, Corgis, Puns. If you like corgis, puns and the dirty dirty jokes of restoration theater, take a class with her!

Lectures were boring but she is an awesome person. I skipped class and didn't read anything until a day before the final, ended up with an 80. REALLY easy final. Papers are worth half of your grade and are not marked by Diana. If you get a bad TA like I did, you're screwed. Luckily my final pulled me up. Would recommend Solomon overall

I absolutely LOVED Diana. She's easily one of my favourite professors at SFU. Her interest and enthusiasm is infectious, and she's always willing to help you. If you have the chance to have her as a professor, go for it! I was fortunate enough to have her as my tutorial leader.   She has a cute corgi too.

Diana was the best prof I had this term. Her passion for English drama during lectures was contagious. Her rubrics clearly outlined the expectations for papers, and the final exam rewarded students who showed up in class. Always kept promises about posting information and grades online. Organized, reasonable and encouraging -- highly recommended!

Really nice lady but the way she marked was insane. Spent a lot of time and effort on my papers and ended up with horrible marks. Also spoke in 90% of tutorials, ended up getting 80100 for participation.. Lecture attendance is highly suggested since that's where she gets the content for her exam. Final was the easiest part of this class.

Prof is really nice but your exam and papers are marked by your TA. Overall pretty easy but if dramas aren't your thing then lectures will be boring. I did minimal studying, didn't read 2 plays and skimmed over the others and ended up with an 87 on the final.

Overall, this class was not hard, quite straightforward. Reall all the reading and understand them, and you will do well. Taking this class I realized that reading and drama isn't for me, thus I found the class to be extremely boring at time.

Nice prof although I usually skip her classes because there're  lecture recordings online. I didn't read most of the plays until the day before final exam, and I still get 80% on final exam. There are no assignment and quizzes, only 2 papers and one final. Overall, this class is an easy W class, definitely recommend it if you need a W credit.

1st semester at SFU and easily she is the worst prof I had so far. Lectures are not useful because she talks about the most common theme around the play that anyone can figure out by themselves. Wastes class time trying to make jokes that are honestly not funny what so ever. Example is her trying to use baseball puns during our lectures.
I agree with what everyone has said. She's fantastic and super passionate about the 18th century. Best English class I've ever taken.

Dr. Solomon's great! She's really renewed my love for literature! She makes her analyses look so easy because she's so understandable. She has a way of explaining the texts so clearly and with such interesting background. She really wants her students to succeed. She's extremely helpful and fun to listen to. And so nice!

Simply put Professor Solomon is cute as a button! She is so passionate about the restoration and 18th century that it is infectious and you can't help but enjoy her lectures even if the subject area isn't your forte.

I LOVE, LOVE, LOVE this prof. SHE IS AWESOME. SO AWESOME. I did not know whether or not I would like the time period that we were studying, but I solely love it because of her. She is refreshing, straight-forward and engaging. She loves what she talks about and she really shows it in her clear voice. This is my favourite class so far. Take this one

Her classes are easy as long as you do your assignments and review the lecture material before exams. Her exams have no surprises. She tells you exactly what's going to be on it and what you have to study. She's pretty anal regarding tutorial attendance though so make sure you don't skip that!

I've taken two courses with this professor so far - a 200-level and 400. She is smart, funny, and passionate. The classes are easy as long as you put a little effort in, but they're fun and enthusiastic. She's more than willing to allow discussion and give help to students who come see her, and is incredibly friendly. I recommend her at all levels!

Diana is more of a shock-trooper of political correctness than a student of literary art. Don't take her classes unless you don't care about exploring how aesthetically strong literature actually works, or if you read with the mindset of an over-sensitive leftist. She is competent in other respects but TOO POLITICAL.

I can honestly say she's the best English prof I've ever had. Knowledgeable, passionate, helpful, engaging, and a fair marker. For the final exam she tells you exactly what you need to study for, so there are no tricks/surprises with her. Definitely reccommend taking a class with her.

Great Prof, knows what she is doing. One of the few classes i didn't find myself falling asleep in. She's very clear with her assignments, and the exam is structured very fairly. TA Creel was awesome too. Great class, great prof, definitely recommend it!!!!
One of the best English profs I've had!  Her lectures are always interesting and well prepared.  I completely recommend her.  :)
Interesting, funny, and definitely knows her stuff. Lectures are engaging and fun. Workload and marking are fair. I recommend this course for anyone interested/majoring in English.

Dr. Solomon is fantastic!  Her lectures and assignments are always clear (not to mention very doable).  She almost invariably tries to inject a little humour into the class, and she has the students participate in class (which can be a huge relief after doing nothing for hours!).   This is the second class I've had with Diana, and both were great.
It's not just about the subject matter. This lady actually like to teach and she's good at it. Also, her hair is awesome...but a bit distracting.
Diana is a delight! She is witty, approachable and kind.
Intro to Drama with Diana is fabulous! So fun, interesting and original. A great introduction to university English
She is a good prof. Doesnt post lec notes so its helpful to come to class. She tests on the stuff she speaks. 2 Papers and a final. Exams are fair/easy. Overall, good class to take with this prof
If you have to take a first year English class, this one was really great.
Liked her a lot!

agree w/ comments both positive & negative... definitely easy, and very generous if you play the gender game. only thing really bugged me... during the primaries made comments about how Obama wasn't qualified. Ok, she's for Hillary, but not qualified? what is up with that?

Diana is obviously passionate about drama. She picked a lot of great plays for us to study in Fall 2008. There were only 2 papers, so the writing part wasn't too bad. She's very approachable and she makes lectures quite interesting. The bad thing is that Diana puts a lot of emphasis on totally trivial details, which can get really annoying.

good prof, nice and approachable. this is her first time teaching engl103 and i personally think she has done a good job at it. she has lecture audio recording available in case u miss her class. the exams are fair, probably can boost ur mark if u suck at essays. assignment, essays or reports, are marked by TAs. so pick a good TA ppl!

felt a little bit like high school... we don't really get into anything in any depth. Just learn her code (gender analysis) and repeat and apply and you will do fine. Whatever you do, don't question the wisdom of only reading plays this ways, not so generous toward diff't outlook

She is an amazing energetic professor.  She wants her students to succeed and will do anything to see all do so.  Reading is a must because a lot of the marks are based on participation (but hey it's an English class!) Great writing exercises.  I have not learned so much in an university class but it was not painful to do so.
